  "findings": [
    "Sling horizontal angle is under 30 degrees, which drastically increases sling tension and risks catastrophic rigging failure."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the lift immediately.",
    "Disassemble the current rigging configuration.",
    "Re-rig using longer slings or a spreader bar to achieve a sling angle of 45 to 60 degrees (minimum 30 degrees per standard)."
  ],
  "rationale": "Rigging with a horizontal sling angle of less than 30 degrees is extremely hazardous because the tension in the slings multiplies rapidly as the angle decreases, exceeding the rated capacity of the rigging hardware. Both ASME B30.9 and OSHA standards prohibit or strongly discourage sling angles below 30 degrees unless specifically approved by a qualified person.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
